$comment: Self-derived finetype drift contract for source.naics, checked by the in-engine finetype_validate@1 gate (the model-free `ft_validate` DuckDB table macro). ft_validate MELTS every column to VARCHAR, so this contract uses ONLY the string-surviving JSON-Schema keywords. ENFORCED: the `enum` closed domain on level; the `pattern` on code — which deliberately admits the official hyphenated sector ranges (31-33, 44-45, 48-49) alongside plain 2-6 digit codes, the exact case finetype's canonical NAICS regex rejects; and `minLength`/`maxLength` envelopes. NOT ENFORCED, by design: numeric ranges (a numeric `type` BACKFIRES to 100% reject once melted to VARCHAR); NULLs (invisible to ft_validate); and object-level `required` (documentation only). Free-text fields (title, description, corpus) carry LOOSE length envelopes with generous headroom (descriptions/corpus run to several KB), not tight formats. Derived from build/naics.parquet: a pass-through on today's 2,125 rows, a fail-closed tripwire for future drift (an unknown level, a malformed code).
title: naics
